<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11395032]] Wikidata name: history of court rank systems in Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=do not know what to title this, I do not think there is a clear good title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:28, 12 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Transitions of the Kani and Ikai Systems''' (冠位・位階制度の変遷) explains the transitions in the {{ill|Rank and Grade|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} system in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
The first unified system to Rank [[:en:Officials|Officials]] in Japan was established in the 11th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (603 CE) with the enactment of the [[:en:Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System]]. In the first year of the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701 CE), the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] introduced the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]], aligning Ranks and [[official positions]]. Although the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] later declined, and the significance of Ranks changed, the format of Ranks itself saw little alteration. With the [[Meiji Restoration]], the traditional Rank and position system was abolished, and a new Rank system was established. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 10 of 1887) severed the link between Ranks and positions, making the system solely for [[:en:commendation|commendation]]. Further refinement occurred under the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 325 of 1926), which solidified the system as one of recognition. Even after the enactment of the [[:en:Constitution of Japan|Constitution of Japan]], the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|[[:en:Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System]]}} In the 11th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|qq=|1=Greater Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|qq=|1=Lesser Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|qq=|1=Greater Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|qq=|1=Lesser Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|qq=|1=Greater Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|qq=|1=Lesser Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|qq=|1=Greater Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|qq=|1=Lesser Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|qq=|1=Greater Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|qq=|1=Lesser Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|qq=|1=Greater Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|qq=|1=Lesser Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|qq=|1=Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|qq=|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|qq=|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|qq=|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|qq=|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|qq=|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|qq=|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|qq=|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|qq=|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|qq=|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|qq=|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|qq=|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|qq=|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|qq=|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with [[:en:Royal family|Royal family]] members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]].
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's [[:en:Constitution|Constitution]], the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * [[:en:Jinshi|Jinshi]] * {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}

Faith|ja|小信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Lesser Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Greater Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Lesser Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Greater Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Lesser Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with [[:en:Royal family|Royal family]] members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]].
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's [[:en:Constitution|Constitution]], the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11395032]] Wikidata name: history of court rank systems in Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=do not know what to title this, I do not think there is a clear good title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:28, 12 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Transitions of the Kani and Ikai Systems''' (冠位・位階制度の変遷) explains the transitions in the {{ill|Rank and Grade|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} system in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
The first unified system to Rank [[:en:Officials|Officials]] in Japan was established in the 11th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (603 CE) with the enactment of the [[:en:Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System]]. In the first year of the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701 CE), the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] introduced the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]], aligning Ranks and [[official positions]]. Although the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] later declined, and the significance of Ranks changed, the format of Ranks itself saw little alteration. With the [[Meiji Restoration]], the traditional Rank and position system was abolished, and a new Rank system was established. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 10 of 1887) severed the link between Ranks and positions, making the system solely for [[:en:commendation|commendation]]. Further refinement occurred under the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 325 of 1926), which solidified the system as one of recognition. Even after the enactment of the [[:en:Constitution of Japan|Constitution of Japan]], the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|[[:en:Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System]]}} In the 11th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|1=Greater Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|1=Lesser Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|1=Greater Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|1=Lesser Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|1=Greater Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|1=Lesser Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|1=Greater Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Lesser Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Greater Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Lesser Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Greater Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Lesser Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with [[:en:Royal family|Royal family]] members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]].
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's [[:en:Constitution|Constitution]], the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * [[:en:Jinshi|Jinshi]] * {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}

The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with [[:en:Royal family|Royal family]] members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]].
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's [[:en:Constitution|Constitution]], the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11395032]] Wikidata name: history of court rank systems in Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=do not know what to title this, I do not think there is a clear good title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:28, 12 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Transitions of the Kani and Ikai Systems''' (冠位・位階制度の変遷) explains the transitions in the {{ill|Rank and Grade|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} system in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
The first unified system to Rank [[:en:Officials|Officials]] in Japan was established in the 11th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (603 CE) with the enactment of the [[:en:Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System]]. In the first year of the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701 CE), the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] introduced the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]], aligning Ranks and [[official positions]]. Although the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] later declined, and the significance of Ranks changed, the format of Ranks itself saw little alteration. With the [[Meiji Restoration]], the traditional Rank and position system was abolished, and a new Rank system was established. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 10 of 1887) severed the link between Ranks and positions, making the system solely for [[:en:commendation|commendation]]. Further refinement occurred under the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 325 of 1926), which solidified the system as one of recognition. Even after the enactment of the [[:en:Constitution of Japan|Constitution of Japan]], the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|[[:en:Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System]]}} In the 11th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|1=Greater Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|1=Lesser Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|1=Greater Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|1=Lesser Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|1=Greater Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|1=Lesser Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|1=Greater Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Lesser Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Greater Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Lesser Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Greater Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Lesser Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with [[:en:Royal family|Royal family]] members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]].
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's [[:en:Constitution|Constitution]], the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * [[:en:Jinshi|Jinshi]] * {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}

These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s 5th year (649 CE). New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with [[:en:Royal family|Royal family]] members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]].
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's [[:en:Constitution|Constitution]], the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.

<!--History of court Rank systems in Japan--><!--History of court Rank systems in Japan--> [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Japanese ranks]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11395032}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11395032]] Wikidata name: history of court rank systems in Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=do not know what to title this, I do not think there is a clear good title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:28, 12 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Transitions of the Kani and Ikai Systems''' (冠位・位階制度の変遷) explains the transitions in the {{ill|Rank and Grade|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} system in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
The first unified system to Rank [[:en:Officials|Officials]] in Japan was established in the 11th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (603 CE) with the enactment of the [[:en:Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System]]. In the first year of the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701 CE), the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] introduced the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]], aligning Ranks and [[official positions]]. Although the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] later declined, and the significance of Ranks changed, the format of Ranks itself saw little alteration. With the [[Meiji Restoration]], the traditional Rank and position system was abolished, and a new Rank system was established. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 10 of 1887) severed the link between Ranks and positions, making the system solely for [[:en:commendation|commendation]]. Further refinement occurred under the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 325 of 1926), which solidified the system as one of recognition. Even after the enactment of the [[:en:Constitution of Japan|Constitution of Japan]], the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|[[:en:Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System]]}} In the 11th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|1=Greater Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|1=Lesser Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|1=Greater Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|1=Lesser Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|1=Greater Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|1=Lesser Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|1=Greater Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Lesser Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Greater Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Lesser Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Greater Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Lesser Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with [[:en:Royal family|Royal family]] members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]].
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's [[:en:Constitution|Constitution]], the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * [[:en:Jinshi|Jinshi]] * {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11395032]] Wikidata name: history of court rank systems in Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=do not know what to title this, I do not think there is a clear good title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:28, 12 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Transitions of the Kani and Ikai Systems''' (冠位・位階制度の変遷) explains the transitions in the {{ill|Rank and Grade|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} system in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
The first unified system to Rank [[:en:Officials|Officials]] in Japan was established in the 11th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (603 CE) with the enactment of the [[:en:Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System]]. In the first year of the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701 CE), the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] introduced the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]], aligning Ranks and [[official positions]]. Although the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] later declined, and the significance of Ranks changed, the format of Ranks itself saw little alteration. With the [[Meiji Restoration]], the traditional Rank and position system was abolished, and a new Rank system was established. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 10 of 1887) severed the link between Ranks and positions, making the system solely for [[:en:commendation|commendation]]. Further refinement occurred under the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 325 of 1926), which solidified the system as one of recognition. Even after the enactment of the [[:en:Constitution of Japan|Constitution of Japan]], the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|[[:en:Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System]]}} In the 11th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|1=Greater Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|1=Lesser Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|1=Greater Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|1=Lesser Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|1=Greater Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|1=Lesser Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|1=Greater Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Lesser Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Greater Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Lesser Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Greater Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Lesser Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with [[:en:Royal family|Royal family]] members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]].
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's [[:en:Constitution|Constitution]], the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11395032]] Wikidata name: history of court rank systems in Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=do not know what to title this, I do not think there is a clear good title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:28, 12 March 2025 (UTC)}}

Although the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] later declined, and the significance of Ranks changed, the format of Ranks itself saw little alteration. With the [[Meiji Restoration]], the traditional Rank and position system was abolished, and a new Rank system was established. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 10 of 1887) severed the link between Ranks and positions, making the system solely for [[:en:commendation|commendation]]. Further refinement occurred under the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 325 of 1926), which solidified the system as one of recognition. Even after the enactment of the [[:en:Constitution of Japan|Constitution of Japan]], the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|[[:en:Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System]]}} In the 11th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|1=Greater Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|1=Lesser Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|1=Greater Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|1=Lesser Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|1=Greater Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|1=Lesser Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|1=Greater Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Lesser Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Greater Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Lesser Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Greater Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Lesser Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with [[:en:Royal family|Royal family]] members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]].
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's [[:en:Constitution|Constitution]], the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11395032]] Wikidata name: history of court rank systems in Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=do not know what to title this, I do not think there is a clear good title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:28, 12 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Transitions of the Kani and Ikai Systems''' (冠位・位階制度の変遷) explains the transitions in the {{ill|Rank and Grade|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} system in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
The first unified system to Rank [[:en:Officials|Officials]] in Japan was established in the 11th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (603 CE) with the enactment of the [[:en:Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System]]. In the first year of the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701 CE), the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] introduced the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]], aligning Ranks and [[official positions]]. Although the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] later declined, and the significance of Ranks changed, the format of Ranks itself saw little alteration. With the [[Meiji Restoration]], the traditional Rank and position system was abolished, and a new Rank system was established. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 10 of 1887) severed the link between Ranks and positions, making the system solely for [[:en:commendation|commendation]]. Further refinement occurred under the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 325 of 1926), which solidified the system as one of recognition. Even after the enactment of the [[:en:Constitution of Japan|Constitution of Japan]], the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.

These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.

New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced.
