Even after the enactment of the {{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|WD=Q237082|lt=Constitution of Japan}}, the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|en|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|WD=Q2448809|lt=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System}}}} In the 11th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|1=Greater Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|1=Lesser Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|1=Greater Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|1=Lesser Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|1=Greater Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|1=Lesser Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|1=Greater Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Lesser Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Greater Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Lesser Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Greater Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Lesser Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku}} (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku}}'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with {{ill|Royal family|en|Royal family|WD=Q2006518|lt=Royal family}} members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code}}.
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's {{ill|Constitution|en|Constitution|WD=Q7755|lt=Constitution}}, the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|WD=Q7408|lt=Imperial Examination}} * {{ill|Jinshi|en|Jinshi|WD=Q420477|lt=Jinshi}} * {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}
{{Court Ranks of Japan}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11395032]] Wikidata name: history of court rank systems in Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=do not know what to title this, I do not think there is a clear good title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:28, 12 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Transitions of the Kani and Ikai Systems''' (冠位・位階制度の変遷) explains the transitions in the {{ill|Rank and Grade|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} system in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.
The first unified system to Rank [[:en:Officials|Officials]] in Japan was established in the 11th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (603 CE) with the enactment of the {{ill|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|en|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|WD=Q2448809|lt=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System}}. In the first year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō era}} (701 CE), the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code}} introduced the {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|WD=Q11452077|lt=Kan'i sōtō system}}, aligning Ranks and [[official positions]]. Although the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} later declined, and the significance of Ranks changed, the format of Ranks itself saw little alteration. With the [[Meiji Restoration]], the traditional Rank and position system was abolished, and a new Rank system was established. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 10 of 1887) severed the link between Ranks and positions, making the system solely for {{ill|commendation|en|commendation|WD=Q41380252|lt=commendation}}. Further refinement occurred under the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 325 of 1926), which solidified the system as one of recognition. Even after the enactment of the {{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|WD=Q237082|lt=Constitution of Japan}}, the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|en|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|WD=Q2448809|lt=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System}}}} In the 11th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|1=Greater Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|1=Lesser Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|1=Greater Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|1=Lesser Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|1=Greater Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|1=Lesser Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|1=Greater Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Lesser Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Greater Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Lesser Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Greater Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Lesser Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku}} (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku}}'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with {{ill|Royal family|en|Royal family|WD=Q2006518|lt=Royal family}} members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code}}.
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's {{ill|Constitution|en|Constitution|WD=Q7755|lt=Constitution}}, the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|WD=Q7408|lt=Imperial Examination}} * {{ill|Jinshi|en|Jinshi|WD=Q420477|lt=Jinshi}} * {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}

In the first year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō era}} (701 CE), the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code}} introduced the {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|WD=Q11452077|lt=Kan'i sōtō system}}, aligning Ranks and [[official positions]]. With the [[Meiji Restoration]], the traditional Rank and position system was abolished, and a new Rank system was established. Further refinement occurred under the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 325 of 1926), which solidified the system as one of recognition. Even after the enactment of the {{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|WD=Q237082|lt=Constitution of Japan}}, the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|en|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|WD=Q2448809|lt=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System}}}} In the 11th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|1=Greater Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|1=Lesser Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|1=Greater Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|1=Lesser Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|1=Greater Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|1=Lesser Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|1=Greater Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Lesser Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Greater Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Lesser Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Greater Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Lesser Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku}} (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku}}'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with {{ill|Royal family|en|Royal family|WD=Q2006518|lt=Royal family}} members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code}}.
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's {{ill|Constitution|en|Constitution|WD=Q7755|lt=Constitution}}, the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|WD=Q7408|lt=Imperial Examination}} * {{ill|Jinshi|en|Jinshi|WD=Q420477|lt=Jinshi}} * {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}
{{Court Ranks of Japan}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]]
'''Transitions of the Kani and Ikai Systems''' (冠位・位階制度の変遷) explains the transitions in the {{ill|Rank and Grade|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} system in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.
The first unified system to Rank [[:en:Officials|Officials]] in Japan was established in the 11th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (603 CE) with the enactment of the {{ill|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|en|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|WD=Q2448809|lt=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System}}. In the first year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō era}} (701 CE), the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code}} introduced the {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|WD=Q11452077|lt=Kan'i sōtō system}}, aligning Ranks and [[official positions]]. Although the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} later declined, and the significance of Ranks changed, the format of Ranks itself saw little alteration. With the [[Meiji Restoration]], the traditional Rank and position system was abolished, and a new Rank system was established. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 10 of 1887) severed the link between Ranks and positions, making the system solely for {{ill|commendation|en|commendation|WD=Q41380252|lt=commendation}}. Further refinement occurred under the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 325 of 1926), which solidified the system as one of recognition. Even after the enactment of the {{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|WD=Q237082|lt=Constitution of Japan}}, the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|en|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|WD=Q2448809|lt=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System}}}} In the 11th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|1=Greater Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|1=Lesser Virtue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|1=Greater Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|1=Lesser Benevolence|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|1=Greater Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|1=Lesser Propriety|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|1=Greater Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Lesser Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Greater Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Lesser Righteousness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Greater Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Lesser Wisdom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku}} (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku}}'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with {{ill|Royal family|en|Royal family|WD=Q2006518|lt=Royal family}} members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code}}.
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Even after the enactment of the {{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|WD=Q237082|lt=Constitution of Japan|1=Constitution of Japan}}, the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|en|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|WD=Q2448809|lt=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|1=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System}}}} In the 11th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|1=Q11435436|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|1=Q11460774|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|1=Q11432288|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|1=Q11458823|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|1=Q11438309|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|1=Q11463290|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|1=Q11432383|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Q11458865|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Q11438510|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Q11463494|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Q11435796|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Q11460880|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Q11353684|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}} (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with {{ill|Royal family|en|Royal family|WD=Q2006518|lt=Royal family|1=Royal family}} members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}}.
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=Court Ranks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's {{ill|Constitution|en|Constitution|WD=Q7755|lt=Constitution|1=Constitution}}, the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|WD=Q7408|lt=Imperial Examination|1=Imperial examination}} * {{ill|Jinshi|en|Jinshi|WD=Q420477|lt=Jinshi|1=Jinshi}} * {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=Court Ranks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]]
'''Transitions of the Kani and Ikai Systems''' (冠位・位階制度の変遷) explains the transitions in the {{ill|Rank and Grade|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} system in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.
The first unified system to Rank [[:en:Officials|Officials]] in Japan was established in the 11th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} (603 CE) with the enactment of the {{ill|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|en|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|WD=Q2448809|lt=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|1=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System}}. In the first year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō era|1=Taiho}} (701 CE), the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}} introduced the {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|WD=Q11452077|lt=Kan'i sōtō system|1=List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles}}, aligning Ranks and [[official positions]]. Although the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} later declined, and the significance of Ranks changed, the format of Ranks itself saw little alteration. With the [[Meiji Restoration]], the traditional Rank and position system was abolished, and a new Rank system was established. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 10 of 1887) severed the link between Ranks and positions, making the system solely for {{ill|commendation|en|commendation|WD=Q41380252|lt=commendation|1=Commendation}}. Further refinement occurred under the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=Court Ranks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Ordinance No. 325 of 1926), which solidified the system as one of recognition. Even after the enactment of the {{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|WD=Q237082|lt=Constitution of Japan|1=Constitution of Japan}}, the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|en|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|WD=Q2448809|lt=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|1=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System}}}} In the 11th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|1=Q11435436|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|1=Q11460774|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|1=Q11432288|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|1=Q11458823|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|1=Q11438309|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|1=Q11463290|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|1=Q11432383|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Q11458865|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Q11438510|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Q11463494|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Q11435796|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Q11460880|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Q11353684|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}} (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with {{ill|Royal family|en|Royal family|WD=Q2006518|lt=Royal family|1=Royal family}} members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}}.
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=Court Ranks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's {{ill|Constitution|en|Constitution|WD=Q7755|lt=Constitution|1=Constitution}}, the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.

Even after the enactment of the {{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|WD=Q237082|lt=Constitution of Japan|1=Constitution of Japan}}, the Order of Rank continues to be used for posthumous recognition.
== Rank Systems == === Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|en|Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|WD=Q2448809|lt=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System|1=Twelve Level Cap and Rank System}}}} In the 11th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} (603 CE), the [[Twelve-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. [[:en:Officials|Officials]] were Ranked into 12 levels: {{ill|Greater Virtue|ja|大徳 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Virtue|1=Q11435436|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435436}}, {{ill|Lesser Virtue|ja|小徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Virtue|1=Q11460774|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460774}}, {{ill|Greater Benevolence|ja|大仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Benevolence|1=Q11432288|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432288}}, {{ill|Lesser Benevolence|ja|小仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Benevolence|1=Q11458823|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458823}}, {{ill|Greater Propriety|ja|大礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Propriety|1=Q11438309|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438309}}, {{ill|Lesser Propriety|ja|小礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Propriety|1=Q11463290|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463290}}, {{ill|Greater Faith|ja|大信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Faith|1=Q11432383|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432383}}, {{ill|Lesser Faith|ja|小信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Faith|1=Q11458865|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458865}}, {{ill|Greater Righteousness|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Righteousness|1=Q11438510|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}, {{ill|Lesser Righteousness|ja|小義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Righteousness|1=Q11463494|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463494}}, {{ill|Greater Wisdom|ja|大智 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Wisdom|1=Q11435796|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435796}}, and {{ill|Lesser Wisdom|ja|小智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Wisdom|1=Q11460880|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460880}}. These Ranks were distinguished by the [[:en:Colors|Colors]] of caps, although the specific colors are not recorded.
=== Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|ja|七色十三階冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Colored Thirteen-Level Cap|1=Q11353684|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353684}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}} (647 CE), the system was revised into the [[Thirteen-Level Cap and Rank System]], implemented the following year (648 CE). This revision incorporated the {{ill|Purple Cap|ja|紫冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purple Cap|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an official Rank. The new Ranks included six additional levels above the previous twelve: {{ill|Greater Woven Cap|ja|大織冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Woven Cap|1=Daishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438508}}, {{ill|Lesser Woven Cap|ja|小織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Woven Cap|1=Shōshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463485}}, {{ill|Greater Embroidered Cap|ja|大繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Embroidered Cap|1=Daishū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438507}}, {{ill|Lesser Embroidered Cap|ja|小繡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Embroidered Cap|1=Shōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463484}}, {{ill|Greater Purple Cap|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Purple Cap|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}, and {{ill|Lesser Purple Cap|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lesser Purple Cap|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}. The lower levels corresponded to the original Ranks.
=== Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System|1=19 Cap Rank System|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395034}}}} Two years after the Thirteen-Level System, it was expanded to the [[Nineteen-Level Cap and Rank System]] in {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}'s 5th year (649 CE). The new system divided the lower levels into subcategories such as {{ill|Upper Greater Flowers|ja|大花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438658}}, {{ill|Lower Greater Flowers|ja|大花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Greater Flowers|1=Daihana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438657}}, {{ill|Upper Lesser Flowers|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}}, and {{ill|Lower Lesser Flowers|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Lesser Flowers|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}. New Ranks like {{ill|Standing Position|ja|立身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Standing Position|1=Risshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598243}} were also introduced.
=== Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System === {{Main|{{ill|Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} (664 CE), the [[Twenty-Six-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were further subdivided, and some names were changed to reflect the intricate Ranking structure. This marked the height of detailed Rank differentiation.
=== Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System === In the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (685 CE), the [[Forty-Eight-Level Cap and Rank System]] was introduced. The Ranks were grouped into categories, with {{ill|Royal family|en|Royal family|WD=Q2006518|lt=Royal family|1=Royal family}} members Ranked above the [[:en:臣|臣]] (vassals). [[Color-coded clothing]] indicated Rank hierarchy. This system lasted until the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}}.
== Modern Rank Systems == === Meiji Period === During the Meiji Restoration, traditional Rank systems were abolished and replaced with modernized structures. The {{ill|Order of Merit|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Merit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1887) separated Ranks from official positions, making them symbolic honors.
=== Order of Rank === In 1926, the {{ill|Order of Rank|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Order of Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=Court Ranks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} codified the system into a posthumous honorary structure. Despite changes in Japan's {{ill|Constitution|en|Constitution|WD=Q7755|lt=Constitution|1=Constitution}}, the Rank system remains a tool of historical recognition.
